canonical_question: What are employment law basics -- contractor vs employee classification, minimum requirements, equity compensation legality?
aliases: How do I classify workers as contractors vs employees for my startup?
What employment laws apply when hiring my first employee internationally?
Is equity compensation legal in my jurisdiction and how do I set it up?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: EU Platform Work Directive effective Dec 2024; UK EMI thresholds expanded Apr 2026; US DOL reverted to economic reality test May 2025

constraints: Worker classification tests differ by jurisdiction -- US IRS common law test, California ABC test, UK IR35, EU Platform Work Directive
Misclassification penalties severe: US $50/W-2 plus 1.5% wages plus 40% FICA; California $5K-$25K per worker
409A valuation required before issuing US stock options
UK EMI requires HMRC valuation within 90 days of grant
Not legal advice -- consult jurisdiction-specific employment counsel

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Only hiring in one country and wants deep-dive
Already has employees and needs classification audit

inputs_needed.question: Where will the startup hire workers?
What type of workers does the startup need?
Does the startup plan to offer equity compensation?
What is the budget for HR/employment platforms?
How many people will be hired in the first 12 months?

related_to.label: Founder compensation and self-employment tax implications
